title: Overcoming State Inertia: Minimally Invasive Temporal Alignment for Evolving Contexts
abstract: arXiv:2512.03704v3 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Long-context dialogue systems suffer from state inertia, where models over-attend to history and fail to adapt to evolving intents. We demonstrate that standard alignment methods like DPO and even recent long-context optimization techniques struggle to resolve this without incurring a severe contextual alignment tax--a substantial perplexity surge caused by disrupting pre-trained priors. To address this, we propose DZ-TiDPO, a minimally invasive framework that synergizes conflict-aware optimization (during training) with a structural temporal attention bias. This design effectively decouples state updating from general linguistic modeling. Experiments on Multi-Session Chat and our new Inertia Challenge (IC-Bench) show DZ-TiDPO preserves structural coherence while resolving inter-turn conflicts. Crucially, our framework supports dual inference strategies: a negligible-latency static mode for general robustness and a precision-focused

Title: Overcoming State Inertia: Minimally Invasive Temporal Alignment for Evolving Contexts
Abstract: arXiv:2512.03704v3 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: Long-context dialogue systems suffer from state inertia, where models over-attend to history and fail to adapt to evolving intents. We demonstrate that standard alignment methods like DPO and even recent long-context optimization techniques struggle to resolve this without incurring a severe contextual alignment tax--a substantial perplexity surge caused by disrupting pre-trained priors. To address this, we propose DZ-TiDPO, a minimally invasive framework that synergizes conflict-aware optimization (during training) with a structural temporal attention bias. This design effectively decouples state updating from general linguistic modeling. Experiments on Multi-Session Chat and our new Inertia Challenge (IC-Bench) show DZ-TiDPO preserves structural coherence while resolving inter-turn conflicts. Crucially, our framework supports dual inference strategies: a negligible-latency static mode for general robustness and a precision-focused
translated_title: 克服状态惯性：面向动态情境的微创时间对齐方法
translated_abstract: arXiv:2512.03704v3 公告类型：替换 摘要：长上下文对话系统存在状态惯性问题，即模型过度关注历史信息而无法适应动态变化的意图。我们证明，标准的对齐方法（如DPO）甚至最近的长上下文优化技术，在解决此问题时都难以避免严重的上下文对齐代价——即因破坏预训练先验知识而导致的大幅困惑度飙升。为解决这一问题，我们提出了DZ-TiDPO，一种微创框架，它将冲突感知优化（训练阶段）与结构性时间注意力偏置协同结合。该设计有效将状态更新与通用语言建模解耦。在多会话聊天数据集及我们新提出的惯性挑战基准（IC-Bench）上的实验表明，DZ-TiDPO在解决跨轮次冲突的同时，保持了结构连贯性。关键的是，我们的框架支持双重推理策略：面向通用鲁棒性的低延迟静态模式，以及面向精确性的模式。
tldr: 本文提出DZ-TiDPO微创框架，通过冲突感知优化与结构性时间注意力偏置协同，有效解决长上下文对话系统的状态惯性问题，避免上下文对齐代价。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes DZ-TiDPO, a minimally invasive framework that synergizes conflict-aware optimization with structural temporal attention bias to effectively overcome state inertia in long-context dialogue systems while avoiding the contextual alignment tax.
